anti_claim: The capsule demonstrates registry-backed JSON-pointer, glob-count, and git-backed callable fact providers with provider failures represented as error rows. It is not a doctrine truth auditor, not a full macro registry export, not semantic claim validation, and not release authority.

anti_claim_contract.rule: Organ-facing public copy must stay within the accepted organ claim ceiling and must not imply release, publication, provider, private-root-equivalence, source-mutation, runtime-correctness, or whole-system-correctness authority.

authority_ceiling.claim_ceiling: Exercises a registry-backed derived fact provider over bounded public fixture roots: it resolves json_pointer (RFC 6901, including list-index traversal), glob_count (with excluded prefixes), and named callable facts (git-tracked counts), and it turns provider failures into error-as-data rows that degrade the receipt status rather than crash the ledger. Positive cases must resolve to the expected values with a clean receipt; negative cases must be rejected by recomputation with the expected provider error_class firing on the planted-defect fact. It is not a doctrine truth auditor, not a full macro fact-registry export, not semantic claim validation, and it does not authorize release or publication.

validation_rules: validate required source refs or target refs remain public-safe
preserve draft/staged status until an active v2 acceptance lane supplies validator and receipt evidence
reject release, provider dispatch, source mutation, private-root equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims
require the accepted organ validator command to recompute the bounded public fixture result
require generated receipts and acceptance receipt refs to remain body-free and public-relative
require claim ceiling, evidence class, truth-accounting bucket, and authority ceiling to match core/organ_registry.json
reject release, publication, provider-dispatch, source-mutation, private-root equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims
receipt_expectations: record validator command identity, observed result status, authority ceiling, and anti-claim
record public-relative receipt refs and avoid source bodies, credentials, provider payloads, private paths, or operator transcripts
record the accepted organ claim ceiling and do not treat validator success as release or publication authority

standard_payload.ontology: Accepted Microcosm organ standard for `derived_fact_provider_runtime`: Exercises a registry-backed derived fact provider over bounded public fixture roots: it resolves json_pointer (RFC 6901, including list-index traversal), glob_count (with excluded prefixes), and named callable facts (git-tracked counts), and it turns provider failures into error-as-data rows that degrade the receipt status rather than crash the ledger. Positive cases must resolve to the expected values with a clean receipt; negative cases must be rejected by recomputation with the expected provider error_class firing on the planted-defect fact. It is not a doctrine truth auditor, not a full macro fact-registry export, not semantic claim validation, and it does not authorize release or publication.
standard_payload.teleology: Expose `derived_fact_provider_runtime` as a bounded public runtime/receipt contract with validator-backed evidence, while keeping the claim ceiling and release boundary visible to cold readers and release gates.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Using `derived_fact_provider_runtime` as proof of broader runtime correctness, release readiness, provider authority, private-root equivalence, or claims outside its accepted organ ceiling.
failure_modes: accepted organ standard file missing or not discoverable as std_microcosm_<organ_id>.json
validator command, receipt id, or authority receipt drifts from core/organ_registry.json
bounded fixture result is trusted from prose or stale receipts instead of recomputed by the validator
public receipt embeds private paths, provider payloads, credentials, raw operator text, or source bodies
evidence class or truth-accounting bucket is overclaimed beyond the accepted organ row
green validator output is treated as release, publication, provider, private-root-equivalence, source-mutation, runtime-correctness, or whole-system-correctness authority
deterministic bounded computation is replaced by fixture echo, schema-only replay, or unverified static metadata

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; accepted organ registry and receipt anchors bind the source row but do not grant release, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, runtime correctness, or whole-system correctness authority.

author.purpose: create a valid Derived fact provider runtime runtime instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Derived fact provider runtime without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_derived_fact_provider_runtime and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections
